title: Using amd64 machine on arm Mac
body:
### Feature request description

Hi, due to https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25641#issuecomment-2783345742 I need another idea of how to run amd64-only Kubernetes workloads. 

The only other idea left is to run an amd64 machine on my Mac, but I can't figure out how to call `machine init`: 

```
$ podman machine init -m 6144  --now --image docker://quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.3-amd64
Looking up Podman Machine image at quay.io/podman/machine-os:5.3-amd64 to create VM
Error: wrong manifest type for disk artifact: application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json
$ podman machine init -m 6144  --now --image docker://quay.io/podman/machine-os@sha256:9390a043921ed8b9e74548b9aa980a61640add64366ebf1a18a48b2724d104da
Looking up Podman Machine image at quay.io/podman/machine-os@sha256:9390a043921ed8b9e74548b9aa980a61640add64366ebf1a18a48b2724d104da to create VM
Error: wrong manifest type for disk artifact: application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json
```

How can I run an amd64 machine on my Mac?

comments.body:
Seems I need to head over to colima for now. Seems to work for me: 

```
$ export QEMU_SYSTEM_X86_64="qemu-system-x86_64 -cpu Haswell-v4"
$ colima start -a x86_64 -c 4 -m 6
```

given that you don't provide the information required, i can only assume you have an arm64 mac and want to run amd64 machines.  That is not supported and is not something in our roadmap.

if you want to run amd64 containers, you can do so with rosetta using the applehv provider as provided in our default distribution.

> given that you don't provide the information required, i can only assume you have an arm64 mac and want to run amd64 machines. 

Exactly. 

> That is not supported and is not something in our roadmap.

Thank you! 

> if you want to run amd64 containers, you can do so with rosetta using the applehv provider as provided in our default distribution.

I tried that, but that doesn't [seem to work for kind containers](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/kind/issues/2738). Unfortunately.

Performance of emulated amd64 on arm in qemu is terrible, dreadful. Might be worth documenting. I haven't been able to set up a stable kind cluster. 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1cf6b733-97af-4c5f-80d9-727d5335efaf)
